assertion_strengthening.denials: Every governed-loop denial now asserts exit code 1, parses the CLI JSON error envelope, checks exact error.code, and requires non-empty error.message. Merge-gate denials additionally require a non-empty remediation_hint with expected terms.
assertion_strengthening.positive_pr_new: Positive `but pr new` paths now assert the command reaches the downstream forge/provider boundary after pull_requests:write authorization, instead of only checking that stderr omits perm.denied.
assertion_strengthening.remediation_rescope: The remediation proof honestly follows the implemented merge-denial hint from the governed feature branch. The structured hint names the reviewed-merge path; the subsequent steps are feature commit, PR creation, governed review approval, and maintainer merge gate reach.

requirement_results.notes: Full implementer, reviewer, maintainer flow is driven through the real but CLI. Positive PR creation requires the forge/provider boundary after gate permit; positive merge requires the forge merge_review boundary after gate permit. Denials parse exact structured JSON.

Remediation uses the implemented merge-denial rescope. The parsed remediation_hint must name `request a reviewed merge`; the test then follows that governed path through feature commit, pr new forge-boundary reach, governed review approve, and maintainer merge-boundary reach.
Dry-run merge by implementer is exact structured perm.denied with remediation_hint and leaves main, source ref, and git object count unchanged.
Auto-merge by implementer is exact structured perm.denied with remediation_hint naming reviewed merge and leaves main unchanged.
Unset and empty BUT_AGENT_HANDLE both fail closed with exit 1, exact structured perm.denied, non-empty message, non-empty remediation_hint, and unchanged main.
Principals use functional permissions only; reviewer and maintainer authorities are group-granted.
Approvals are submitted only through governed `but review approve`; no direct DB/raw git bypass assertion is introduced.

fakeability_red_evidence.notes: Records the strengthened assertion failures observed before helper repair: wrong field placement for remediation_hint, too-narrow PR forge-boundary marker, and parser rejection of the one-line Error JSON envelope.

anti_stub_fakeability_notes: The tests invoke the real `but` binary through Sandbox::but for commits, pr new, review approve, pr merge, pr merge --dry-run, and pr auto-merge.
Governance config is committed into real refs via the sandbox git/bash fixture before CLI commands run.
Denial assertions parse the CLI JSON envelope instead of matching raw substrings.
Positive PR-open paths prove gate permit by requiring downstream forge/provider-boundary reach.
Positive merge paths prove gate permit by requiring downstream forge merge_review-boundary reach.
Reviewer approvals are submitted through governed `but review approve`; no local_review_verdicts write appears in the capstone test file.
The local review cache is seeded only with ForgeReview metadata so the real CLI can select review 77 in a local no-forge fixture.
